methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Neustark is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Valentin Gutknecht and Johannes Tiefenthaler, having raised $70M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of nine firms in the same sector and stage: eight private companies and one public (Lanzatech, LNZA). The peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), among others.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $70M raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies $133M. Peer-median analysis across six same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and commercial traction within frontier energy.

The two-method triangulation produces a median estimate of $244M, bounded by a low of $113M (conservative case anchored to stage progression) and a high of $408M (peer-median-weighted upside). Key uncertainties include technology validation progress relative to peers, capital intensity of scaling, and competitive positioning within carbon capture and emissions reduction—factors not fully captured by valuation multiples alone.
